zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Cailly Lord of Massingham於大約1135年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可格亞特 馬辛加姆時，他的父親Simon De Cailly Lord of Massingham11133歲，母親Alice de Massingham16歲。他於1160年之前與Beatrice在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1199年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Cailly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French:
topographic name for a person living on a patch of pebbly or stony soil, from Old French caillou ‘pebble, stone’, a word of Celtic origin. Compare Lacaille 2.

variants.name: Currier
meanings: English: occupational name for a person who dressed leather after it was tanned, from Middle English curr(e)iour (Old French conreeur ‘currier’).
Americanized form of French Caillé (see Caille 3) and Carrière (see Carriere ).

variants.name: Lacaille
meanings: French:
metonymic occupational name for someone who caught and sold quails, a variant of Caille 2, with fused feminine definite article la.

topographic name for a person living on a patch of pebbly or stony soil, a variant of Caille 1, with fused feminine definite article la; or a habitational name from La Caille, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Lacoy .

variants.name: Caye
meanings: French (mainly Lorraine): possibly a topographic name from Old French caie ‘sandbank, dike’.
French: nickname from dialect caye ‘sow’.
Possibly also an altered form of French   Caille   or   Caillet   (or of some other like-sounding surname); compare   Caya  .

variants.name: Cayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adelard, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Lucien, Andre, Benoit, Emile, Fernand, Germaine, Jean-Marc, Jean-Marie.
Altered form of French Caillé (see Caille 3). Compare Cayea .
French: from Old French caier, a term denoting a pack of four candles (from Latin quaterni), possibly applied as a metonymic occupational name for a candle merchant.

variants.name: Caya
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Aurore, Cecile, Girard, Jacques, Normand.

Altered form of French Cailla, itself a dialect form of Caille 2 ‘quail’ (or perhaps an altered form of some other similar surname beginning in Caill-) or a habitational name from a minor place called Cailla. Compare Cayo .

variants.name: Caillet
meanings: French:

topographic name for a person living on a patch of pebbly or stony soil, or a habitational name from (Le) Caillet, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a diminutive of   Caille   1.
derivative of   Cail   3 ‘curds’, a metonymic occupational name for a dairy worker.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Cailly Lord of Massingham was born about 1135, in Great Massingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Simon De Cailly Lord of Massingham, was 11133 and his mother, Alice de Massingham, was 16. He married Beatrice before 1160, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died before 1199.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Cailly Lord of Massingham est né environ 1135, à Great Massingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Simon De Cailly Lord of Massingham, avait 11133 ans et sa mère, Alice de Massingham, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice avant 1160, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé avant 1199.

Lui sposato Beatrice prima del 1160, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 1199.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Cailly Lord of Massinghamが約1135年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクグレアト マシンガムで生まれた時，父Simon De Cailly Lord of Massinghamは11133歳，母Alice de Massinghamは16歳であった。1160年以前にイギリスでBeatriceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1199年以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Cailly Lord of Massingham(이)가 대략 1135년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 그리트 마징함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon De Cailly Lord of Massingham(은)는 11133세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Massingham(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1160년 이전 영국에서 Beatrice(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1199년 이전 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Cailly Lord of Massingham родился около 1135 г. в местности грит массингхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Simon De Cailly Lord of Massingham было 11133 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Massingham, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice перед 1160 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер перед 1199 г.
